steps.title: Identify the sequential structure
steps.content: The passage uses “first” and “next” to order the dyeing steps. The blank requires a transition to mark the final step.

steps.title: Match the transition to complete the sequence
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the sentences is sequence. The passage uses "first" and "next" to order the dyeing steps; the blank introduces the final step. The blank needs a transition signaling the last item in a sequence.
  • Choice A — "Finally,": signals the last step in an ordered sequence, fitting perfectly after "first" and "next." ✓
  • Choice B — "However,": signals contrast — the final step would oppose the prior step. Removing cloth from a dye bath is not a contradiction of simmering it. ✗
  • Choice C — "Therefore,": signals cause-effect — removing the cloth would be a logical result of the prior sentence alone. While removing follows simmering, the transition needed is sequential (ordinal), not causal. ✗
  • Choice D — "Actually,": signals a correction or surprising fact that contradicts an expectation. No correction is being made here. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
